title: Canadian food inflation, energy, and the exchange rate
description: Food prices may respond to international commodities, energy, currency, domestic supply, processing, and margins; co-movement is not causal proof.
plainLanguageSummary: Food prices may respond to international commodities, energy, currency, domestic supply, processing, and margins; co-movement is not causal proof.
howToRead: Compare direction, turning points, and sustained divergences. Use the tooltip for exact values and observation dates; visual alignment alone does not establish causation.

calculation.formula: Published indices and explicitly labelled derived changes; no interpolation across native frequencies.
calculation.explanation: Food prices may respond to international commodities, energy, currency, domestic supply, processing, and margins; co-movement is not causal proof.
calculation.example: A value at date t uses only observations dated t or earlier unless the chart explicitly labels a visual shift.

patternsToWatch: Turning points or divergences that repeat across historical episodes
limitations: Food prices may respond to international commodities, energy, currency, domestic supply, processing, and margins; co-movement is not causal proof.

methodology.formula: Published indices and explicitly labelled derived changes; no interpolation across native frequencies.
methodology.notes: Food prices may respond to international commodities, energy, currency, domestic supply, processing, and margins; co-movement is not causal proof.
